turns.content: I thought I could just link my application against the standard library and then jump to it in memory, but I keep getting compilation errors because I'm missing the core trait implementations.

That's a common mistake for beginners who assume embedded Rust works exactly like desktop development. You cannot use std::vec or other library features directly because they require an operating system that doesn't exist on bare metal; instead, you must implement the core traits like impl core::fmt::Debug yourself and ensure your crate is compiled with no_std flags. Think of it like building a house without electricity first: you can't plug in appliances (standard library functions) until you've laid the wiring (bootloader logic) to power them on.
